hero_tagline: Planning your family getaway to the stunning Merimbula area means finding a holiday park that perfectly matches your crew's vibe, whether you're after beachside adventures or peaceful bushland retreats.
heading: Best Family Holiday & Caravan Parks in Merimbula
hero_intro:
Planning a family trip to Merimbula? We compared the best family holiday and caravan parks in Merimbula so you can quickly find the right park for your kids, budget and travel style.
Merimbula sits on the Sapphire Coast with a lake, an ocean beach and oysters straight from the water, and Discovery Parks Pambula Beach brings genuine kangaroo encounters into the mix.
local_knowledge: Discovery Parks Pambula Beach is the standout, with a heated indoor pool, outdoor waterpark slides and regular kangaroo sightings around the grounds, a genuine drawcard for kids who've never seen one up close. Merimbula Lake offers calm swimming for younger children, an easier alternative to the ocean beach when the swell is up. The Merimbula boardwalk lets you walk over the lake entrance, and the Merimbula wharf's aquarium gives a rainy-day option with local marine life. Oyster tastings straight from the farms are a genuine Sapphire Coast specialty, and Tathra's beach, 20 minutes south, is worth the drive.

if_we_were_booking.label: Kangaroos and waterpark
if_we_were_booking.text: We'd choose Discovery Parks Pambula Beach for the heated indoor pool, outdoor waterpark slides and the genuine chance of kangaroo encounters around the park.

if_we_were_booking.label: Lake swimming
if_we_were_booking.text: We'd choose Merimbula Lake Holiday Park for calm, sheltered water suited to younger children.

if_we_were_booking.label: Our tip
if_we_were_booking.text: Walk the Merimbula boardwalk over the lake entrance at low tide, then finish with fresh oysters from one of the local farms. It's the quintessential Sapphire Coast afternoon and works for kids who like the walk and adults who like the oysters.

faq.question: Is Merimbula good for families?
faq.answer: Yes, especially with young kids drawn to wildlife. Discovery Parks Pambula Beach combines pool facilities with regular kangaroo sightings.

faq.question: Which Merimbula park is best for kids?
faq.answer: Discovery Parks Pambula Beach, with a heated indoor pool, outdoor waterpark and organised kids activities.

faq.question: Will kids really see kangaroos?
faq.answer: Sightings around Discovery Parks Pambula Beach are common, though as with all wildlife, not guaranteed every visit.

faq.question: Is Merimbula Lake safe for young children?
faq.answer: Yes, it's generally calm and shallow near the shore, a good alternative to the ocean beach for younger swimmers.

faq.question: What is the Merimbula boardwalk?
faq.answer: A walkway over the lake entrance near the town centre, an easy flat walk with good views, especially at low tide.

faq.question: Can you try local oysters with kids?
faq.answer: Yes, several local farms offer tastings, and even fussy eaters often enjoy the novelty of seeing where oysters actually come from.

faq.question: What are the best free family activities?
faq.answer: The boardwalk, Merimbula's main beach, and the wharf area for fishing and watching the boats.

faq.question: When is the best time to visit?
faq.answer: Summer for swimming, though the Sapphire Coast is pleasant across most of the year given its mild climate.

faq.question: What should families do when it rains?
faq.answer: The Merimbula wharf aquarium offers an indoor option with local marine life on display.

faq.question: How far ahead should you book?
faq.answer: Discovery Parks Pambula Beach books out for school holidays given its popularity, so book ahead.
